title: Shinji a hero
wikitext:
[]: Shinji, a hero?             Quote Post by GAP » Thu Nov 26, 2009 3:50 pm I originally didn't want to start a new thread but I have a question, what if Shinji became the hero everyone wanted? What if he did have a harem with Misato, Asuka and Rei? I ask this because the reason I enjoyed Shinji's character so much because he was so real and not some shonen hero stereotype like Naruto or Goku but he is a farcry from the useless male lead like I see in harem anime nowadays. I truly like this Shinji, he may not be a badass but he gets the job done anyway in spite of his fears but at the same time, I see myself in him than any other character in the show because he reminds of my own failings and how I should confront them, not run from them. Would Evangelion be as popular as it is if we had a heroic Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Nov 26, 2009 4:01 pm It would depend on how exactly events played out around him, what legacy AU-Evangelion left. There are a ton of other super-robot series, some of which have more staying power than others -- which other super-robot would AU-Evangelion be? Ideon? RahXephon? Big O? GaoGaiGar? ... or perhaps something else not yet attempted?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Nov 26, 2009 4:04 pm Shinji has faced the darkest depths of human despair, and returned to tell the tale. He is a hero for us all. EDIT: I can't spell.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Nov 26, 2009 4:08 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Shinji has faced the darkest depths of human despair(...)

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Nov 26, 2009 4:10 pm Allemann wrote: ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Shinji has faced the darkest depths of human despair(...)

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Nov 26, 2009 4:19 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Allemann wrote: Far from it. Trust me.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Nov 26, 2009 4:26 pm Allemann wrote: Eva Yojimbo wrote: Allemann wrote: Far from it. Trust me.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Nov 26, 2009 4:37 pm Xard wrote: if we go by certain dialogues in ep 25 he has :P

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Nov 26, 2009 4:45 pm Allemann wrote: His worst problem are social awkwardness, lack of self-esteem, sexual frustration, and a lousy father.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Nov 26, 2009 4:53 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: (...) considering Shinji's circumstances I'm inclined to think he's in a much worse position than what any philosophers were ever in.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Nov 26, 2009 5:04 pm oh, right. Kierkegaard. Nvm then... Allemann wrote: Eva Yojimbo wrote: (...) considering Shinji's circumstances I'm inclined to think he's in a much worse position than what any philosophers were ever in.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Nov 26, 2009 5:12 pm Allemann wrote: Maybe you should start reading them.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Nov 26, 2009 5:22 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Seriously, if you would choose to be Shinji in that situation (sure, pilot the biological robot with your mother's soul to save the world against monsters trying to kill you while working with your father who abandoned you; and he dares complain - what a spoiled brat) instead of Nietzsche (whom I'm inclined to think that any philosopher would be way more spoiled than Shinji; most regular people have to worry about actually surviving instead of having all that time to philosophize about stuff) then you are one twisted individual.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Nov 26, 2009 5:42 pm Erm, how is Shinji a loser because of his circumstances, exactly? I think you have a skewed view of Shinji or the series based on your posts in this thread. >_>;

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Nov 26, 2009 5:49 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Erm, how is Shinji a loser because of his circumstances, exactly? I think you have a skewed view of Shinji or the series based on your posts in this thread. >_>;

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Nov 26, 2009 6:02 pm Oh, look! It's this thread again! TOPIC, BITCHES! And just a reminder Topic = GAP wrote: Would Evangelion be as popular as it is if we had a heroic Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Nov 26, 2009 6:04 pm EDIT: I posted the rest of my reply to Allemann in that thread Tines linked to. The series' popularity if Shinji had been a normal hero is a very difficult thing to assess since so much of the series is built around him being an anti-hero and much of its provocation and lasting impact is precisely because of Anno's subversion of the hero archetype. But NGE's first half had gotten so much hype and popularity that I'm inclined to believe that if the second half had been of the same quality but much more straight-forward then it would probably have still been popular. But I believe its lasting popularity is due to all the ways in which it breaks the mold; including Shinji being the anti-hero. So I would say that it would've been popular, but wouldn't have near the staying power.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Nov 26, 2009 6:04 pm Shinji literally had everything he ever loved, cared about, or believed in destroyed infront of his eyes, and one could make a very good argument that it was entirely his fault even when there was absolutely nothing he could do, trapping him in a catch-22 of moral accountability. Also, using Eternal Recurrence is fucking stupid because even if true, Shinji wouldn't remember anyway, and therefore not effecting the tragedy of his situation in any way ever. To say Shinji has not faced the darkest depths of human despair is to greatly misunderstand the human condition and what real suffering is. It's the same as calling Shinji some whiny emo bitch, ironically making the accuser the one who looks like a spoiled brat. As has come up in threads before, I have had a VERY shitty life and have been pushed to suicidal tendencies that I only escape because of what I consider to be a true miracle. Even if I didn't have that, Shinji is still, still, still waaaaay worse off than I am.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Nov 26, 2009 6:07 pm AuraTwilight wrote: I have had a VERY shitty life and have been pushed to suicidal tendencies that I only escape because of what I consider to be a true miracle. Even if I didn't have that, Shinji is still, still, still waaaaay worse off than I am.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Nov 26, 2009 6:41 pm He's my hero In all seriousness, though, AuraTwilight sums up my feelings exactly.

I ask this because the reason I enjoyed Shinji's character so much because he was so real and not some shonen hero stereotype like Naruto or Goku but he is a farcry from the useless mal
